author: sungdark
title: Python Version Compatibility, Build Backend, Excessive Type Annotations
notes: All three claims are invalid. (1) NotRequired requires Python 3.11, but the project explicitly declares requires-python>=3.11 — using stdlib typing features for the targeted version is correct engineering. (2) Claim that setuptools doesn't support editable installs is factually wrong — setuptools>=68 fully supports pip install -e. (3) 'Excessive type annotations' is a subjective style complaint, not a structural engineering issue. TypedDict with NotRequired is standard Python typing for the project's target version.
